recapPrompt.prompt: Why can saving a 5-dollar bill be better than using three of them right away in Lemonade Change?

choices.label: Because 5-dollar bills are more flexible for future change
choices.feedback: Correct. The greedy rule protects the bill that helps the most future customers.

choices.label: Because it returns more money
choices.feedback: Not this one. Both ways return the same total amount.

reflectionPrompt.prompt: Explain in your own words why some greedy problems can be solved with just one best-so-far value.
reflectionPrompt.placeholder: They can be solved that way because...

practiceChallenge.title: Mastery check: rebuild the greedy clues
practiceChallenge.description: Complete the helper so it returns the main clue for matching greedily, keeping best reach, and resetting after a failed start.
hints: Assign Cookies uses the smallest helpful match.
Jump Game keeps the farthest reach so far.
Gas Station resets the start after the running tank fails.
practiceChallenge.successMessage: Pass. You rebuilt the main greedy clues from memory.
practiceChallenge.retryMessage: Needs practice. Recheck matching, farthest reach, and reset rules.

blanks.label: Main clue for best reach
expectedValues: keep the farthest reach so far

blanks.label: Main clue for reset-after-failure problems
expectedValues: reset to the next start after failure
